programmes.show_description: Jacqueline, assisted by Kenneth and the visiting Troy, scatters her late husband's ashes over Benidorm. S8 E1
When Terry is asked to help remove some squatters by a friend of Arthur's, it seems the friend's granddaughter might not be all she seems. S2 E8: All Mod Cons
Alf tries the patience of good natured Fred Johnson and his wife from next door by using their phone to make a very long-winded long distance call to Rita. S1 E4
Robin has taken his new girlfriend Angie out on a couple of dates, and now he hopes to bring her back to the flat to have his wicked way with her. S3 E2: Come Into My Parlour
Several months have passed - Amy is working, but David is still unemployed and lies to her that his doctor has told him he will have a nervous breakdown unless he takes a holiday. S3 E1: Winter Break

programmes.show_name: Journeys: Isle of Man Steam Rail
programmes.show_description: Opened in 1873, the Isle of Man Steam Railway is the longest narrow gauge steam line in Britain that uses its original locomotives and carriages. We journey from Douglas to Port Erin.

Dogs are only allowed to travel on the top deck of a bus; when they are as big as Aunt Maud's pet, it is best that they not travel at all... S2 E4: Aunt Maud
The Minister for Transport is on his way to visit the station, but disaster awaits as there is no milk left at the train's buffet for his cup of tea! S2 E1: No Milk For The Minister
Jack is convinced that something is going on between May and Cecil - and with good reason as they knew each other 18 years earlier and May happens to have an 18-year-old daughter. S2 E2: Father's Day
Jack finds a bag stuffed full of money and has grand plans for a life of luxury, but they soon fall flat. S2 E3: The Gravy Train
British Railways issues Mr Parkin with a van for his job, but he needs to learn how to drive it. S2 E4: The Van
There is talk of a wildcat strike among drivers - if any Hatley staff join it, the station could be closed. S2 E5: Lucky Strike
Classic sitcom about one-man disaster area Frank Spencer. Frank finds it hard to believe that the council has condemned their old house. S3 E1: Moving House
Classic sitcom featuring the accident-prone Frank Spencer and his long-suffering wife. Frank's attempt at building Jessica a doll's house in woodwork class gets out of hand. S3 E2: Wendy House
Classic sitcom. When Betty comes home after a dance lesson with their neighbour, Frank becomes suspicious of his intentions and insists on getting involved. S3 E3: Scottish Dancing
Classic sitcom featuring Frank Spencer, a well-meaning and enthusiastic one-man disaster area. Frank's grandad arrives from Australia to find his grandson in drag. S3 E4: Men as Women
Classic '70s sitcom. Frank's local labour exchange puts him to work as a motorbike courier. S3 E5: King of the Road
The Spencers visit Australia House to discuss their possible emigration, and Frank takes elocution lessons to prepare for his flying lessons. S3 E6: Australia House
Harold has been offered a job elsewhere, so when he discovers Albert has been drinking out of the cocktail cabinet, he begins to pack up his belongings and leave his dad for good. S1 E1: The Offer
Harold has been going out in the evenings and making Albert suspicious - it turns out that Harold has a 'bird', so Albert suggests that he invite her round. S1 E2: The Bird
Harold has holiday plans and they don't involve Albert or the annual trip to Bognor Regis; Albert attempts to change his son's mind by using emotional blackmail. S1 E6: The Holiday
Hercules the horse is sick and the Steptoes' business is under threat; fellow totters rally around and Albert and Harold hatch a plan to profit. S2 E1: Wallah-Wallah Catsmeat
Arthur gets involved with a small-time crook who manages to steal a suitcase filled with money - and soon realises that the notes are suspect. S1 E3: The Smaller They Are...
As Arthur attempts to ingratiate himself with members of the local golf club, DS Morley offers to sell him a police transport coach. S8 E12: The Coach That Came In From The Cold
Joyce has been let down by her first-aid instructor and must find someone else to train the staff - Mateo? S8 E2
Karen and Jamie's relationship is rocked when Karen has a miscarriage, and the Maguires' reaction to losing a 'grandson' makes things worse. S6 E5
Jo sees a mouse in her bedroom and refuses to sleep in there; much to Chrissy's dismay, Jo swaps with Robin while the mouse is on the loose. S3 E4: Of Mice And Women
Alan's getting serious about Caroline, but her parents are well-to-do and Rigsby is far from convinced that the young scruff could possibly make the relationship work. S3 E6: The Cocktail Hour
Joyce has been let down by her first-aid instructor and must find someone else to train the staff - Mateo? S8 E2
When Rose Mellors is back on the scene, it can only mean trouble; what is she doing with bags full of diamonds, and why does she need Terry to drive her? S2 E9: Diamonds Are a Girl's Worst Enemy
Rita comes to visit which causes friction between Alf and Else. However, when Alf criticises Margaret Thatcher the women gang up on him. S1 E5
There is a formal dinner dance at the firm where Jo and Chrissy work, but Chrissy's dance partner has injured himself and so Robin becomes her partner instead. S3 E3: I Won't Dance, Don't Ask Me
David is caught out when Amy tells him that Tommy Wright is dead, so he and Linda adopt disguises to avoid each other's spouses as they plan to move to another hotel. S3 E2: Deja Vu

When Stan is courting, it leads him into trouble at work; Jack tries to help him out, but conductors don't always know when to stop. S2 E5: Late Again
The pilot episode of the 'upstairs downstairs' sitcom by the writers of Hi-de-Hi! Alf and his daughter Ivy enter service at the Meldrum house, but Alf gets a shock after seeing a familiar face.
Ethel has fallen in love with an American pilot called Joe, but Wilfred is not at all pleased. S2 E6: Love is a Very Splendid Thing
The annual bowls club outing seems to provide an ideal opportunity for Cecil to be alone with May. S2 E7: A Bowl In The Hand
The station is prepared for a visit by the chairman of British Railways, but it turns out to be a different Doctor Beeching altogether. S2 E8: Action Stations
Dorien's book group is convening to discuss Crime and Punishment, which turns out to be rather applicable to the events that unfold in Tracey's household. S11 E5: Tracey's Choice
Tracey is out of prison, and Sharon gets a job as a school dinner supervisor and recruits Garth as her cook and Tracey as her washer-up. S11 E6: The Chief, The Cook, His Mum and Her Lodger
Sharon makes plans to take Tracey and Dorien on a madcap '70s weekender, but there is no dressing up soe news that Tracey is about to receive. S11 E7: Without a Trace
Tracey and Sharon take a trip to a spa to cope with Tracey's shattering news and try to make plans for the future, and Dorien prepares to say a long goodbye to her daughter. S11 E8: Spa Wars
Albert decides to get married again, but despite the qualities of his future stepmother, Harold does everything to put a stop to it. S2 E3: The Stepmother
Harold takes Albert on a big night out in the West End to celebrate his birthday, but the night soon turns into a disaster with no beer and horrible food. S2 E4: Sixty-Five Today
Harold has acquired a collection of classical music records and plans a cultural evening; Albert hates it so much that he takes some drastic action. S2 E5: A Musical Evening
Harold has arranged a poker game with a few friends; Albert is morally outraged by the idea of gambling, but he sees that Harold is being cheated by his friends and decides to help. S2 E6: Full House
Harold falls for a wealthy married woman while out on his rounds; Albert is unimpressed and tries to talk Harold out of making a fool of himself. S2 E7: Is That Your Horse Outside?
Terry is assigned to be a bodyguard for a Lebanese businessman who is so nervous that he insists on carrying a gun - will it come in handy? S1 E4: The Tethered Goat
When a young widow is conned out of all of her savings, Arthur sends Terry off to reclaim the money - is there anything he can hold hostage to bait the thief? S1 E5: The Bounty Hunter
Having been robbed of his winnings and barred from every casino, Maurice finds a way of getting rich again and needs Terry's help. S1 E6: Aces High and Sometimes Very Low
When their van breaks down, Arthur and Ray take a cargo of dodgy videos onto a barge travelling through the glorious waterways of England. S8 E13: The Cruel Canal
The Maltbys are back and Pauline must battle her demons. Tiger and Joey get into trouble with a biker gang. S8 E3
Paddy wakes up disorientated and chained to a bed in a strange room. Enter Maureen, the woman he's been secretly seeing since they met in the graveyard, and things seem to become a little clearer.
Chrissy receives flowers and chocolates from a secret admirer; it turns out that they were meant for Jo and that the admirer had wrongly assumed her to be Chrissy. S3 E5: Somebody Out there Likes Me
The house is plunged into confusion and mourning when Rigsby discovers basement tenant Mr Osborne stiff as a board. S3 E7: Suddenly At Home
